title: 有翼之暗
summary:
Mochizuki presents a new hypothesis to the police chief and the detective regarding the killer's identity. He initially suspected the killer might be illiterate due to the simple hiragana used in the threatening letters left by Kanae and Mikari, which only contained the words "secret" and "2 o'clock." However, Mochizuki notes that the killer's ability to imitate detective Kiyoshiro Kishiro and their use of the "Lecchonawa Book" (a Bible translation) proves they can read. He concludes that the killer is not illiterate, but rather "illiterate in Japanese," meaning they can only read English. This points toward Mizuho, a foreigner who lived in America until five months ago.

Mochizuki also resolves the mystery of the threatening letter sent to Hagino. He determines that neither the murderer nor a third party sent it; instead, Yudu sent the letter to Hagino to manipulate her temperament and ensure she would come to the Inari house. This is supported by the fact that Yudu had spilled glue on his desk while preparing the letter.

Finally, Mochizuki identifies the murderer as Kiri, describing her as a genius who treated the mansion and its inhabitants like pieces in a crossword puzzle. He begins to recount the Ito-Hyoma murder case, noting that Ito had approached them with a request. He explains that around 3 am, Mukemizu struck UMAYama from behind with a blunt object, causing him to faint. Mukemizu then moved the unconscious UMAYama into Ito's room, intending to carry out an execution based on the "Lecchonawa Book" and a painting in Kanayan's house, acting as if she were a reincarnation of God.
